note: Three follow-up fixes on top of v182, all in website/sost-wallet.html. (1) P1: TEMPLATE_NAMES_JS was used in the Decrypt Sealed Capsule panel without being defined in this file (it lived in sost-explorer.html). A successful Sealed Structured decrypt would have crashed the panel with a ReferenceError before the && short-circuit. The wallet now defines its own TEMPLATE_NAMES_JS map (mirrors the explorer), and the use site additionally guards with `typeof !== 'undefined'`. (2) P2: _openSealedEnvelope had _libsecpEcdh and _hkdfSha256 outside the try/catch — a malformed ephemeral pubkey would throw past the AES-GCM catch and surface as an unhandled rejection. The full crypto path (ECDH → HKDF → AES import → AES decrypt) is now wrapped in a single try/catch that returns either bad_ephemeral_pub or auth_failed; both render as the grey 'not decryptable by this wallet' chip. (3) Fee loop in sendTransaction now converges from a realistic minimum seed (1 input + 2 outputs + capsule) instead of seeding at 148 × min(N, 256) inputs which over-collected fee on small payments from large wallets. The loop steps the fee straight to txSize × feePerByte (4 passes max, then a final exact-fee rebuild) and refuses to broadcast if it cannot converge. NO consensus, NO RPC, NO transaction wire format, NO node, NO miner. Force-reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
